contract.name: v2/bulk_download/awards.md
contract.description: Starts a bulk award download job and returns URLs plus the sanitized payload used to build the archive.

filters.description: Primary filter payload sent to the download generator.
filters.constraints: required; must be a JSON object; server rejects non-object values.

filters.prime_award_types.description: Codes for prime award types to include.
filters.prime_award_types.constraints: enum members ['IDV_A','IDV_B','IDV_B_A','IDV_B_B','IDV_B_C','IDV_C','IDV_D','IDV_E','02','03','04','05','06','07','08','09','10','11','A','B','C','D','-1']; at least one item required when sub_award_types absent; empty array or unknown codes return 400.

filters.sub_award_types.description: Codes for subaward types to include.
filters.sub_award_types.constraints: enum members ['grant','procurement']; at least one item required when prime_award_types absent; empty array or unknown codes return 422/400.

filters.date_type.description: Determines which transaction date field is filtered.
filters.date_type.constraints: enum ['action_date','last_modified_date']; invalid values return 400; requirement status when omitted is unknown (see gaps).

filters.date_range.description: Inclusive bounds for the filtered date field.
filters.date_range.constraints: required; must include start_date and end_date strings; missing object returns 422.

filters.date_range.start_date.constraints: values earlier than 1980-01-01 triggered upstream WAF resets; 1980-01-01 and later accepted.

filters.date_range.end_date.constraints: must be on or after start_date; reversed ranges trigger WAF HTML block.

filters.agencies.description: Optional list of agency filter objects.
filters.agencies.constraints: optional; each object must include type ('funding'|'awarding'), tier ('toptier'|'subtier'), and name; missing name triggered WAF HTML block.

filters.keyword.constraints: optional; accepted but removed from stored filters in responses.

filters.keywords.description: List-based keyword filter used by the UI.
filters.keywords.constraints: optional; accepts array of strings; values are omitted from download_request.filters.

filters.place_of_performance_scope.description: Filter for domestic or foreign places of performance.

filters.place_of_performance_locations.description: Place of performance filters using the shared location schema.
filters.place_of_performance_locations.constraints: optional; each object must include country; missing country returns 422 with required-field list.

filters.recipient_scope.description: Filter for domestic or foreign recipient scope.

filters.recipient_locations.description: Recipient location filters using the shared schema.
filters.recipient_locations.constraints: optional; each entry must include country; missing country returns 422.

columns.description: Optional list of requested column identifiers.
columns.constraints: optional; accepts [] or null; arbitrary strings are accepted; non-array non-null values return 400.

file_format.description: Requested file format for generated CSV/TSV/PSTXT files.
file_format.constraints: optional; default 'csv'; enum ['csv','tsv','pstxt']; invalid values return 400.

status_url.description: URL to poll download job status.

file_name.description: Zip filename assigned to the generated archive.

file_url.description: Direct HTTPS link to the generated archive under files.usaspending.gov.

download_request.description: Echo of the sanitized request used to create the job.

download_types.description: Job types that will be generated (e.g., ['prime_awards'], ['elasticsearch_sub_awards']).

file_format.description: File format queued for the job.

columns.description: Echoed column list when provided; omitted when null.

filters.description: Normalized filters; includes prime_and_sub_award_types, time_period, and any accepted location or scope filters.

body.detail: Missing one or more required body parameters: prime_award_types or sub_award_types

quirks: Request must include prime_award_types or sub_award_types despite documentation marking them optional.
Backend rewrites filters into prime_and_sub_award_types/time_period and drops keyword/keywords inputs from the stored payload.
columns values are only type-checked; unknown column names are accepted without validation.
Upstream WAF returns HTML 500 pages or connection resets for certain validation issues (reversed date ranges, missing agency names, or start dates before 1980).
risks: Repeated invalid requests or early date ranges can trip the WAF and temporarily block subsequent calls.
Asynchronous jobs can produce large files; clients must poll status_url and handle sizable downloads.
Requesting invalid column names may yield unusable output files because the server does not validate column identifiers.
gaps: Unclear whether filters.date_type defaults when omitted; omission behavior not probed.
Exact mapping between malformed agency objects and WAF blocks versus structured JSON errors remains unknown.

probes.notes: Prime award request succeeds and returns status/file URLs plus normalized filters.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Missing one or more required body parameters: prime_award_types or sub_award_types"}

probes.notes: Server enforces presence of prime_award_types or sub_award_types.
Reversed date ranges trip the upstream WAF and return an HTML block page.

response.bodyExcerpt: Connection closed without HTTP response (curl exit 52)

probes.notes: Start dates before 1980 caused the upstream WAF to drop the connection entirely.

mismatches: Documentation marks filters.agencies as required, but probes show successful calls without it.
Documentation lists prime_award_types as optional, yet the API demands prime_award_types or sub_award_types.
Documentation implies keyword values are preserved, but the service drops keyword/keywords from download_request.filters.
gaps: Unclear whether filters.date_type defaults when omitted; omission behavior not probed.
Exact mapping between malformed agency objects and WAF blocks versus structured JSON errors remains unknown.
risks: Repeated invalid requests or early date ranges can trip the WAF and temporarily block subsequent calls.
Asynchronous jobs can produce large files; clients must poll status_url and handle sizable downloads.
Requesting invalid column names may yield unusable output files because the server does not validate column identifiers.
